violations.text: There was no person in charge certified in food safety present as needed at the time of inspection. Owner is to ensure a person certified in food safety is present at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025

Food items prepared on site and frozen for later use were observed not re-marked with proper date of use excluding the time it was frozen. Date of preparation is to be counted as day 1 with 6 days remaining from the day of defrosting. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025

The following food items were observed improperly cooling... - Muffin mix, prepared same day, was observed cooling in the glass door cooler unit at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. (Based on time and temperature, Muffin mix was moved to colder refrigeration for rapid cooling at the time of inspection.) - Bacon was observed cooling in the cold drawer unit at 62 degrees Fahrenheit. Bacon is to be cooled in colder refrigeration prior to being placed in the cold drawer unit to prevent possible loss of temperature control of other food items in the unit. (Bacon was moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients were observed not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025
The faucet to the hand sink in the kitchen was observed to be dripping/leaking when turned off. Equipment is to be kept in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Shelving - Refrigeration surfaces - Cooking equipment surfaces Equipment is to be cleaned as often as necessary to preclude the buildup of debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

The wall behind the Freezer Drawer unit was observed to be in poor repair (Taped/covered with plastic). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2025

The Wall/Ceiling juncture where the kitchen meets the service area was observed to have exposed wood. Wood is to be covered/sealed to ensure it remains smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2025

Raw animal foods (Whole Shell Eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the glass door reach-in low boy cooler refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Eggs were arranged to be stored properly at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

Wood is used in the construction of counter in the front service area. The wood is observed to be untreated/unsealed. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2024

The Wall/Ceiling juncture where the kitchen meets the service area was observed to have exposed wood. Wood is to be covered/sealed to ensure it remains smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2024

At the time of inspection, an allergen awareness statement was not available for customer viewing. An allergen awareness statement is to be viewable by the customers at all times. (Per the PIC, statement to be added to the menu board.) New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the surfaces of kitchen equipment between units and shelving were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2023

The Wall/Ceiling juncture where the kitchen meets the service area was observed to have exposed wood. Wood is to be covered/sealed to ensure it remains smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2023
